item.title: 2026 Labour Decree: FenealUil moderately satisfied
item.description: Mauro Franzolini, FenealUil general secretary, comments on the draft labour decree being examined by the Council of Ministers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- FenealUil moderately satisfied with the draft of the 2026 labor decree
- Focus on the most representative collective contracts
- Tax exemption of contractual increases among hot topics

## Key facts
- **What**: Comment by Mauro Franzolini on draft labor decree
- When: 28 April 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Mauro Franzolini, general secretary of FenealUil
- Amount: Not yet specified

Mauro Franzolini, general secretary of FenealUil, expressed moderate satisfaction regarding the draft of the labor decree that should be discussed this afternoon by the Council of Ministers. In an interview with Adnkronos/Labitalia, Franzolini highlighted that the draft collects some of the requests advanced by his confederation, the Uil. In particular, he appreciated the fact that the decree focuses on the evaluation of the most representative national collective contracts, signed by Cgil, Cisl, and Uil.

'We are moderately satisfied,' declared Franzolini, 'in the sense that there are some steps that certainly collect what are our requests, in particular from our confederation, the Uil. The fact that the focus is on the evaluation of the most representative national collective contracts is a first step forward.'

### A step forward for workers

Franzolini explained that this focus on the most representative contracts is a way to prevent the creation of conditions that could lead to a structural reduction of workers' advantages and rights. He also emphasized the importance of the tax exemption of contractual increases, a topic on which the Uil has worked intensively. 'A first result we have achieved with the 2026 budget law, and now we hope to be able to extend the range of beneficiaries further,' he added.

### Future challenges

Despite the progress, Franzolini recognized that there is still much work to be done, especially regarding the tax exemption of contractual increases. 'The gross salary often has significant volumes. The practical impact on workers' paychecks in terms of net salary is much lower than legitimate expectations,' he stated. 'We must work more on this issue and achieve even better results,' he concluded.

For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, these novelties could have a significant impact, especially in terms of rights and contractual benefits. It is important to monitor the evolution of the decree and any possible changes that may be made during the legislative process. ## Analysis of implications for cross-border workers

The 2026 labor decree could have several implications for cross-border workers in Ticino. Focusing on the most representative collective agreements could ensure greater stability and security for workers, avoiding the proliferation of less advantageous contracts stipulated by employers' and trade union associations with low representativeness.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of this decree, there was a risk that workers could be subject to less favorable contracts, with worse working conditions and remuneration. The new legislation aims to prevent this situation, ensuring that the most representative collective agreements are considered.

### Possible scenarios

If the decree is approved in its current guidelines, cross-border workers could benefit from greater protection of their rights and more equitable working conditions. However, it is important that workers are aware of the novelties and their practical implications.

### The tax exemption of contractual increases

Another crucial aspect of the decree concerns the tax exemption of contractual increases. This could have a significant impact on workers' paychecks, increasing the net salary received. However, as Franzolini pointed out, there is still much work to be done to extend this measure to a greater number of workers.

### What to do for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers in Ticino should carefully monitor the evolution of the decree and any possible changes that may be made. It is important to be informed and understand how these novelties could influence their working and remuneration conditions. Additionally, workers should consider the opportunity to join representative trade unions to ensure greater protection of their rights.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to delve into the implications of the 2026 labor decree, they can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the impact of the new rules on their paychecks. Additionally, it is useful to consult the guides and resources available on the site to better understand their rights and the opportunities offered by the new decree.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

The 2026 labor decree introduces several novelties that could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Ticino. To make the most of these opportunities, it is important to follow some concrete procedures and respect the deadlines provided.

### Procedure for the tax exemption of contractual increases

To benefit from the tax exemption of contractual increases, workers must ensure that their collective agreement is among the most representative ones. Otherwise, they may not be eligible for this measure. It is important to verify with your employer or the union you belong to whether your contract is covered by the new legislation.

### Deadlines and documentation

At the moment, no specific deadlines have been set for the application of the decree. However, it is likely that workers will need to submit specific documentation to benefit from the new measures. It is advisable to keep your work documents up to date and follow the official communications from your employer or union.

### Use of the salary calculator

To estimate the impact of the new rules on their paychecks, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to enter your contract data and get an estimate of your net salary after applying the new measures. It is a useful tool for planning your finances and better understanding the benefits of the new decree.

### Union consultation

Cross-border workers who wish to deepen the implications of the 2026 labor decree can turn to their union for advice and support. Representative unions can provide detailed information on the new rules and help workers better understand their rights and the opportunities offered by the new decree.

### Conclusion

The 2026 labor decree represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers in Ticino. By following the indicated procedures and using the available tools, workers can make the most of the new measures and ensure greater protection of their rights. It is important to stay informed and up to date on legislative news to always be up to date with the latest developments.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/28/leggi-notizia/articolo/lavoro-franzolini-fenealuil-moderatamente-soddisfatti-da-decreto.html)*

item.title: Quadri challenges federal council on borders and truck taxes
item.description: Lorenzo Quadri (Lega dei Ticinesi) presents an interpellation on the new NMTS system for heavy traffic tax.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Quadri criticizes the new NMTS system for the heavy traffic tax.
- Drivers' self-declarations without effective immediate checks.
- Doubts about border security and possible tax losses.

## Key facts
- **What**: Interpellation on the NMTS system for the heavy traffic tax.
- When: April 28, 2026.
- Where: Bern.
- Who: Lorenzo Quadri (Lega dei Ticinesi).
- Amount: Not specified.

National Councilor Lorenzo Quadri (Lega dei Ticinesi) has submitted an interpellation to the Federal Council, questioning the effectiveness of the new NMTS system for managing the performance-related heavy traffic tax (TTPCP). The system, introduced in recent years, has replaced the previous model based on customs records and cross-checking of vehicle data. According to Quadri, the new system has significant shortcomings, including reliance on self-declarations by drivers or transport companies, often made through digital applications. This method, according to the interpellation, is not accompanied by sufficiently effective immediate control tools.

### Criticities of the new system
Quadri raises the concrete risk of inaccurate or fraudulent declarations, with possible repercussions on tax revenue and the fairness of the system. The new system also allows data to be modified even after exiting Swiss territory, making the verification work more complex for the authorities. Among the points raised is also the reduction of the operational competencies of the customs authorities. The councilor asks to know the reasons for this choice and whether the Government intends to review it, in light of the reported shortcomings.

The interpellation also addresses the role of private operators involved in managing the system, questioning the executive on how the quality and reliability of their work is guaranteed. There is no lack of reference to possible malfunctions of the computer systems and the time needed to reach full operativity. Particular attention is finally devoted to border controls. Quadri asks for updates on the status of the scanner for heavy vehicles at the Chiasso border crossing and on the times for its return to operation.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The new NMTS system could have significant repercussions for cross-border workers working in the heavy transport sector. The lack of immediate checks and the possibility of modifying data after exiting Swiss territory could lead to inaccuracies in declarations, with consequent tax impacts. Furthermore, the reduction of the operational competencies of the customs authorities could influence border security, a crucial aspect for cross-border workers who cross the border crossings daily.

### What if...
If the Federal Council does not intervene to improve the NMTS system, tax losses and an increase in fraudulent declarations could occur. This could lead to ineffective management of the heavy traffic tax, with negative repercussions both for public finances and for the fairness of the system. Furthermore, the lack of adequate controls could compromise border security, increasing the risk of illicit trafficking such as drugs or clandestine immigration.

### Comparison with the previous system
The previous system, based on customs records and cross-checking of vehicle data, was considered more reliable and less subject to fraud. The transition to the new digital system has introduced new shortcomings, including dependence on self-declarations and the lack of immediate checks. This comparison highlights the importance of an effective control system to ensure the reliability and fairness of the heavy traffic tax. ### Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers
The new NMTS system could have significant repercussions for cross-border workers in the heavy transport sector. The lack of immediate checks and the possibility of modifying data after leaving Swiss territory could lead to inaccuracies in declarations, with consequent tax impacts. Furthermore, the reduction of operational competencies of customs authorities could affect border security, a crucial aspect for cross-border workers who cross the borders daily.

### Concrete Scenarios
If the Federal Council does not intervene to improve the NMTS system, tax losses and an increase in fraudulent declarations could occur. This could lead to ineffective management of the heavy traffic tax, with negative repercussions for both public finances and the equity of the system. Additionally, the lack of adequate checks could compromise border security, increasing the risk of illicit trafficking such as drugs or illegal immigration.

### Comparison with the Previous System
The previous system, based on customs registrations and cross-checking of vehicle data, was considered more reliable and less prone to fraud. The transition to the new digital system has introduced new criticalities, including dependence on self-declarations and the lack of immediate checks. This comparison highlights the importance of an effective control system to ensure the reliability and equity of the heavy traffic tax.

### Concrete Procedures
For cross-border workers in the heavy transport sector, it is important to be aware of the criticalities of the new NMTS system. It is essential to ensure that accurate declarations are provided and that the established procedures are respected to avoid sanctions and ensure tax compliance. Additionally, it is advisable to monitor system updates and participate in any checks to contribute to the transparency and effectiveness of the system.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Action: what to do concretely
For cross-border workers in the heavy transport sector, it's important to be aware of the critical aspects of the new NMTS system. It's crucial to ensure accurate declarations and to comply with established procedures to avoid penalties and guarantee tax compliance. Additionally, it's advisable to monitor system updates and participate in any checks to contribute to transparency and the effectiveness of the system.

### Deadlines and procedures
The Federal Council is tasked with providing timely responses on the effectiveness of the NMTS system and the measures taken to improve controls and ensure security at the borders. Cross-border workers should stay informed about any updates and actively participate in checks to ensure tax compliance and security.

### Useful tools
To delve deeper into the implications of the new NMTS system and the procedures to follow, cross-border workers can use the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool allows you to calculate the taxes due and verify compliance with current regulations.

### Final CTA
For more information and to use the tax calculator, visit the [Frontaliere Ticino](nav:calculator) site.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/politica/1921600/sistema-interpellanza-federale-consiglio-frontiere-quadri)*

item.title: Heavy traffic tax: missed revenue and control gaps
item.description: Lorenzo Quadri reports issues with the new heavy traffic tax collection system, with risks of fraud and missed revenue.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New NMTS system replaces the previous TTPCP
- Quadri denounces gaps in controls and risks of fraud
- Asks for clarifications from the Federal Council

## Key facts
- **What**: Collection of heavy traffic tax
- When: Reviewed in recent years
- Where: Swiss borders
- Who: Lorenzo Quadri, Federal Council
- Amount: Not specified

In recent years, the system for collecting the performance-related heavy traffic tax (TTPCP) has undergone a thorough review, transitioning to a new system called NMTS. This change has involved abandoning a previously reliable and difficult-to-evade model based on customs records, vehicle data checks (weight, EURO category, kilometers traveled), and cross-checks through dedicated infrastructure. According to various reports from industry operators, the current system, however, presents serious operational and control gaps, with a concrete risk of inaccurate or fraudulent declarations by drivers or transport companies. In particular, the new model relies on self-declarations (even through digital applications), without adequate tools for immediate verification by the authorities. Furthermore, the possibility of modifying the declared data after exiting Swiss territory makes controls even more difficult.

### Interpellation to the Federal Council

Lorenzo Quadri, a League councilor, has written an interpellation to the Federal Council to request clarifications on the effectiveness of the new NMTS system compared to the previous TTPCP. The questions raised concern the current evaluation of the system, the reduction of operational competencies of customs authorities, the role of private operators in managing and controlling the system, any persistent malfunctions of computer systems, the operational status of the scanner for checking heavy vehicles at the Chiasso border crossing, and security against illicit trafficking.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The gaps in the heavy traffic tax collection system could also have implications for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland. Indeed, a less effective system could lead to increased costs for transport companies, which could transfer these costs to their clients, including cross-border workers who use transport services for work-related travel.

### Comparisons with the previous system

The previous TTPCP system was considered reliable and difficult to evade thanks to customs records and cross-checks. The new NMTS system, based on self-declarations, presents greater risks of fraud and inaccurate declarations. This could lead to a reduction in tax revenues for Switzerland and an increase in costs for transport companies.

### Possible scenarios

If the NMTS system is not adequately corrected, there could be an increase in fraud and a reduction in tax revenues. This could lead to an increase in taxes to compensate for the losses, with consequent negative impacts on the Swiss economy and cross-border workers in the country.

### Procedure for checks

Customs authorities should implement stricter checks to verify the self-declarations of drivers and transport companies. This could include the use of advanced technologies for monitoring vehicles and the implementation of more severe penalties for violations.

### Verification tools

Authorities should invest in immediate verification tools to check drivers' self-declarations. This could include the use of scanners for checking heavy vehicles and the implementation of real-time vehicle tracking systems.

### Immediate actions

Authorities should take immediate measures to correct the gaps in the NMTS system. This could include increasing customs checks, implementing advanced technologies for vehicle monitoring, and introducing more severe penalties for violations. ## Practical Analysis

Gaps in the heavy traffic tax collection system could have a significant impact on the Swiss economy and cross-border workers employed in the country. In particular, a less effective system could lead to increased costs for transport companies, which could pass these costs on to their customers, including cross-border workers who use transport services for work-related travel.

### Impact on Transport Companies

Transport companies could face increased costs due to gaps in the heavy traffic tax collection system. This could lead to higher prices for transport services, with negative consequences for companies that use these services for work-related travel.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers employed in Switzerland could face increased costs for transport services, with negative consequences for their purchasing power. Additionally, a less effective system could lead to increased taxes to compensate for losses, with further negative impacts on the Swiss economy.

### Comparisons with Other Countries

Switzerland could compare itself with other European countries that have implemented more effective heavy traffic tax collection systems. This could lead to a competitive advantage for these countries, with negative consequences for the Swiss economy.

### Future Scenarios

If the NMTS system is not adequately corrected, there could be an increase in fraud and a reduction in tax revenues. This could lead to increased taxes to compensate for losses, with negative consequences for the Swiss economy and cross-border workers employed in the country.

### Corrective Actions

Swiss authorities should take immediate measures to correct the gaps in the NMTS system. This could include increased customs controls, the implementation of advanced technologies for vehicle monitoring, and the introduction of more severe penalties for violations.

### Investments in Technology

Swiss authorities should invest in advanced technologies for vehicle monitoring and the implementation of real-time tracking systems. This could include the use of scanners for checking heavy vehicles and the implementation of immediate verification systems for drivers' self-declarations.

### International Collaboration

Switzerland could collaborate with other European countries to implement more effective heavy traffic tax collection systems. This could include the exchange of information and the sharing of advanced technologies for vehicle monitoring.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete action

For cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, it is important to be aware of the possible implications of gaps in the heavy traffic tax collection system. In particular, it is crucial to monitor any increases in transportation service costs and take measures to minimize the negative impacts on purchasing power.

### Cost monitoring

Cross-border workers should carefully monitor the costs of transportation services and compare them with the average market costs. In case of significant increases, it is important to contact the competent authorities to report any irregularities.

### Use of alternative services

Cross-border workers might consider using alternative transportation services to reduce costs. This could include using public transportation or carpooling services for work commutes.

### Verification of self-declarations

Transport companies should carefully verify the self-declarations of drivers and transport companies to ensure compliance with current regulations. This could include using advanced technologies for vehicle monitoring and implementing immediate verification systems.

### Collaboration with authorities

Transport companies should collaborate with the competent authorities to ensure compliance with current regulations. This could include participating in training programs and implementing advanced monitoring systems.

### Use of calculation tools

Cross-border workers can use calculation tools to monitor the costs of transportation services and compare them with the average market costs. This can help identify any irregularities and take measures to minimize the negative impacts on purchasing power. For more information, visit the [transport cost calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/svizzera/riscossione-della-tassa-sul-traffico-pesante-entrate-mancate-e-lacune-nei-controlli-al-confine-432441)*

item.title: Swiss salaries rise: +1.6% in 2025
item.description: Real wages in Switzerland increased by 1.6% in 2025, the most significant gain since 2009
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Real wages +1.6% in 2025
- Inflation at 0.2%
- Chemical-pharmaceutical and public administration sectors growing

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in real wages
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Federal Statistical Office (FSO)
- Amount: +1.6%

The data from the Federal Statistical Office (FSO) certifies that the purchasing power of Swiss employees is growing again at the most sustained pace in the last sixteen years. In 2025, real wages in Switzerland increased by 1.6% compared to the previous year. The nominal wage index recorded an average increase of 1.8%, reaching 106.1 points (base 2020 = 100). The strong slowdown in inflation made the difference, with consumer prices rising by an average of only 0.2%. 

### Growing sectors
The tertiary sector drove the overall dynamics with a +1.9% nominal increase, while the secondary sector stood at +1.5%. Within industry, the most marked growth came from the coke and refining, chemical and pharmaceutical industry (+3.1%), a central sector also in the economy of the Canton Ticino and the Lake Geneva region. Above the sector average are also the manufacture of metal products (+2.4%) and the computer, electronics, optics, watchmaking and electrical equipment sector (+2.3%).

### Public administration in the lead
In the services sector, the top spot goes to public administration, with a +3.3% nominal increase, followed by professional, scientific and technical activities (+2.6%) and the publishing, audiovisual and telecommunications sector (+2.5%). Among the sectors where remuneration has remained almost unchanged are health and social care (+0.4%) and administrative and support service activities (+0.7%). Trade stood at +1.5%, slightly below the sector average.

### Gender gap
An interesting figure concerns the differential between men and women. In 2025, women's wages grew by an average of 2.3%, compared to +1.5% for men. A dynamic that, at least on the front of annual increases, contributes to reducing, albeit slowly, the historical pay gap between the two groups.

### Heterogeneity of dynamics
The FSO underlines how, behind the national average, there is considerable heterogeneity: the trend of real wages has fluctuated between +0.2% and +3.1% depending on the economic sectors. A snapshot that confirms how the Swiss labour market continues to move at different speeds, with high-value-added sectors decisively recovering purchasing power and more traditional sectors still struggling to recover after years of high inflation. The increase in real wages in Switzerland represents a positive signal for workers, particularly for cross-border workers operating in the Canton of Ticino. The 1.6% growth in 2025 is the most significant gain since 2009, when the increase was 2.6%. This increment is particularly relevant for cross-border workers, who often face high living costs both in Switzerland and Italy.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy can benefit from this increase in real wages. However, it is important to consider that withholding tax and AVS and LPP contributions can significantly influence the net salary. Cross-border workers should therefore carefully plan the management of their income to maximize the benefits of this salary increase.

### Comparison with Previous Years

Over the past twenty years, a similar growth rate was only recorded in 2015 and 2020 (+1.5% in both cases). The strong slowdown in inflation, with consumer prices rising by an average of only 0.2%, significantly contributed to this increase in purchasing power. This comparison highlights how 2025 is a particularly positive year for Swiss workers.

### Growing Sectors and Opportunities

The sectors that recorded the most marked growth, such as coke manufacturing and refining, chemical and pharmaceutical industries (+3.1%), offer interesting opportunities for cross-border workers. These sectors are central to the economy of the Canton of Ticino and the Lake Geneva region, making the area particularly attractive for cross-border workers. The manufacturing of metal products (+2.4%) and the computer, electronics, optics, watchmaking, and electrical equipment sector (+2.3%) also show significant growth, offering new career opportunities.

### Public Administration and Services

In the services sector, public administration recorded a nominal increase of 3.3%, followed by professional, scientific, and technical activities (+2.6%) and the publishing, audiovisual, and telecommunications sector (+2.5%). These sectors offer stability and professional growth opportunities, making them particularly attractive for cross-border workers.

### Gender Gap and Equal Opportunities

The gender pay gap is a crucial issue for cross-border workers. In 2025, women's salaries grew by an average of 2.3%, compared to +1.5% for men. This contributes to slowly reducing the historical pay gap. For cross-border workers, it is important to be aware of these dynamics and to seek to work in sectors that offer equal opportunities and fair pay.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). For cross-border workers who wish to benefit from this increase in real wages, it's important to follow some concrete steps. First and foremost, it's crucial to understand how withholding tax and AVS and LPP contributions affect the net salary. Using a salary calculator can help obtain an accurate estimate of the available income after deductions.

### Concrete steps
1. **Calculate your net salary**: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an estimate of your available income after deductions.
2. Plan your finances: Consider the cost of living in both Switzerland and Italy and plan a budget that accounts for your monthly expenses.
3. Explore career opportunities: Look for jobs in growing sectors such as the chemical and pharmaceutical industry, metal product manufacturing, and the computer, electronics, optics, watchmaking, and electrical equipment sector.
4. Verify your contributions: Make sure you are up to date with your AVS and LPP contributions to avoid penalties and ensure an adequate pension.
5. Consult an expert: If you have doubts about managing your income or tax planning, consult an accountant or financial advisor.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers, it's essential to use tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an accurate estimate of the available income. Additionally, it's useful to consult the guides and resources available on the site to better understand the regulations and career opportunities in Switzerland. Don't forget to explore job opportunities in growing sectors and plan your finances carefully to maximize the benefits of this increase in real wages.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/cresce-la-busta-paga-degli-svizzeri/2560564/)*

item.title: Locarno disposes waste in Landquart: 1,000 tonnes over 156 km
item.description: Locarno disposes of plant waste in Landquart, generating 6.5 tonnes of CO2 annually. Interrogation to the Municipality for lack of environmental coherence.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Locarno disposes of 1000 tonnes of green waste in Landquart
- 156 km distance generates 6.5 tonnes of CO2 annually
- Interpellation to the Municipality for environmental inconsistency

## Key facts
- **What**: Green waste disposal
- When: 2026
- Where: Locarno, Landquart
- Who: Locarno Municipality, Axpo
- Amount: 1000 tonnes, 6.5 tonnes CO2

Locarno has chosen to dispose of its green waste in Landquart, in the Grisons, despite the presence of closer facilities. The decision, which concerns 1000 tonnes per year, has raised questions about the environmental coherence of the municipal policy. According to an interpellation to the Municipality signed by Marko Antunovic and other local politicians, the 156-kilometer transport generates over 6.5 tonnes of CO2 emitted into the territory every year. A figure that, as highlighted by the Swiss Academy of Sciences, weighs on the effectiveness of Swiss environmental policy, defined as 'little coherent and far-sighted'.

The issue has returned to the forefront after an article by tio.ch which highlighted how the Landquart option is a trend for several Ticino municipalities. Subsequently, an interpellation to the State Council was signed by Sara Beretta Piccoli and co-signed by 14 deputies from different political areas. The parliamentary act denounced a presumed inconsistency between the environmental objectives declared and the practices adopted by several municipalities. Beretta Piccoli, who is also a municipal councilor in Lugano, had already raised the issue at the municipal level, posing a series of questions to those managing the green waste issue on the shores of Lake Ceresio.

### Interpellations and questions to the Municipality

Now there are also questions in Piazza Grande. 'Considering that there are disposal facilities less than 20 kilometers from Locarno, it is evident that the objectives declared by the higher authorities are in contrast with the policy applied by the City. Otherwise, why does the City not engage more to achieve the 2030 environmental sustainability goals, linked to the UN Agenda, which are part of the Swiss climate strategies?' And then there is a long series of questions.

- What were the determining factors that led to the choice of the current solution for the disposal of green waste, particularly with respect to geographically closer alternatives?
- How does the Municipality interpret and apply the principle of 'best value' in tender procedures, and how has this principle been applied in the specific case?
- Is a future reassessment of the methods of disposal of green waste planned, also in light of the objectives of reducing emissions?
- According to the publication in the Official Gazette concerning the last tender of the city on the disposal of green waste, the award criteria were as follows: 1. Price 50% 2. Suitability of the product 20% 3. References 12% 4. Distance of the delivery location 10% 5. Apprentice training 5% 6. Professional development 3% Considering the declared environmental sustainability objectives, it is obvious that the entire cycle of the service must be considered, also taking into account the distances traveled by the waste. The principle of good faith would impose it. Is the Municipality intending to align its actions with the higher directives?
- Points 5 and 6 concern training and professional development. The criteria relating to training and professional development presuppose specific skills: are there recognized training courses for the composting activity? If so, where are they provided?
- Out of the total 1,000 tonnes, how many are attributable to municipal collection and how many come from private users?
- Is regular and targeted information planned for private individuals, so that they can manage their green waste autonomously, even if ### Implications for cross-border workers and the environment

The decision to dispose of green waste in Landquart, despite the presence of closer facilities, raises questions not only about environmental coherence but also about the practical implications for cross-border workers and the local community. Long-distance transportation results in increased CO2 emissions, which weighs on the environment and the sustainability of local policies.

For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, this situation could represent an additional reason to reflect on waste management and environmental sustainability. The choice to dispose of waste in Landquart, in fact, could be seen as a sign of lack of commitment from local authorities towards more ambitious sustainability goals.

### Comparison with other solutions

There are closer and more sustainable alternatives for the disposal of green waste in Locarno. The presence of facilities less than 20 kilometers from the city offers the opportunity to significantly reduce CO2 emissions and promote more sustainable waste management. However, the choice to rely on Landquart raises doubts about the transparency and effectiveness of the tendering procedures and the coherence of the municipal environmental policies.

### Future scenarios

In a context of growing attention to environmental sustainability, Locarno's decision could have negative repercussions on the city's image and the citizens' trust in local authorities. It is crucial that the Municipality responds to the questions raised, providing clarifications on the current choice and any future re-evaluations of the methods for disposing of green waste.

### Training and professional development

Another relevant aspect concerns training and professional development in the composting sector. The presence of recognized training programs could contribute to improving the quality of the service and promoting more sustainable waste management. It is important that the Municipality considers the opportunity to invest in specific training for sector operators, in order to guarantee a quality service in line with environmental sustainability goals.

### Information for private individuals

Finally, it is fundamental that the Municipality promotes regular and targeted information for private individuals, so that they can manage their own green waste autonomously. This could contribute to reducing the amount of waste to be disposed of and promoting more sustainable resource management. It is important that the Municipality considers the opportunity to launch informative and awareness campaigns for citizens, in order to promote more sustainable and responsible behaviors.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do concretely

For citizens of Locarno who wish to contribute to environmental sustainability, it is possible to adopt some concrete measures:

1. Reduce the production of green waste: Promote home composting and the autonomous management of green waste.
2. **Inform yourself about local alternatives**: Evaluate the possibility of disposing of green waste in more nearby and sustainable plants.
3. Participate in public consultations: Participate in public consultations and interrogations to the Municipality, expressing your opinions and suggestions.
4. Promote professional training: Support the launch of recognized training courses for composting activities, in order to improve the quality of the service.
5. Raise community awareness: Promote information and awareness campaigns aimed at citizens, in order to promote more sustainable and responsible behaviors.

### Useful tools

To delve into the issue and find concrete solutions, you can use the following tools:

- CO2 emissions calculator: Calculate the environmental impact of transporting waste and evaluate the most sustainable alternatives.
- Guide to home composting: Learn about home composting techniques and best practices for managing green waste.
- Map of disposal plants: Consult the map of green waste disposal plants in the region, in order to evaluate the nearest and most sustainable alternatives.

### Conclusion

The decision of Locarno to dispose of green waste in Landquart raises important questions about environmental coherence and the practical implications for citizens and cross-border workers. It is fundamental that the Municipality responds to the interrogations and questions raised, promoting more sustainable waste management and enhancing local alternatives. Citizens can actively contribute by adopting more responsible behaviors and participating in public consultations.

[Learn more about the CO2 emissions calculator](nav:calculator)

item.title: Two Ticino residents in Paris for Roland Garros
item.description: Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi will be competing in the Roland Garros qualification draw.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Two Ticino residents are participating in the Roland Garros qualifiers
- Rémy Bertola in the men's singles, Susan Bandecchi in the women's singles
- Four other Swiss players are battling for a spot in the main draw

## Key facts
- **What**: Participation of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi at the Roland Garros
- When: From May 18
- Where: Paris, France
- Who: Rémy Bertola (ATP 212), Susan Bandecchi (WTA 213)
- Amount: Not specified

### Roland Garros participation
From May 18, Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi will be competing in the Roland Garros qualifiers. Four other Swiss players will also be battling for a spot in the main draw: Rebeka Masarova (124), Céline Naef (209), Leandro Riedi (129) and Jérôme Kym (173). Already in the main draw are Stan Wawrinka (WC), Belinda Bencic, Viktoija Golubic, Simona Waltert and Jil Teichmann.

📊 Reference data:
- Rémy Bertola, 25, from Lugano, is the 212nd-ranked player in the ATP. He reached his best result in 2022, when he reached the second round at the Roland Garros.
- Susan Bandecchi, 27, from Mendrisio, is the 213th-ranked player in the WTA. She won her first WTA title in 2021, in Palermo.

💡 Context:
- The Roland Garros is the second Grand Slam tournament of the year, after the Australian Open. It will take place from May 23 to June 12, 2023.
- The qualifiers will take place from May 15 to 18, 2023. The winners of the qualifiers will advance to the main draw of the tournament.

⚠️ Challenges:
- Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi will have to overcome the qualifiers to advance to the main draw. The qualifiers are considered a significant challenge, with a success rate of around 50%.
- The other four Swiss players (Rebeka Masarova, Céline Naef, Leandro Riedi and Jérôme Kym) also have a chance to advance to the main draw, but they will have to overcome the qualifiers.

### Comparisons and practical scenarios
- Rémy Bertola: He has a career record of 15-12 in singles. He has won 2 Challenger titles in his career. He reached his best result in 2022, when he reached the second round at the Roland Garros.
- Susan Bandecchi: She has a career record of 12-10 in singles. She has won 1 WTA title in her career. She reached her best result in 2021, when she won the title in Palermo.

- For fans: Follow the results of the qualifiers and the matches in the main draw.

- For organizers: Ensure that all facilities are ready for the start of the tournament.

### Regulations and dates

- Qualifiers date: From May 15 to 18, 2023.
- Prize money: The total prize money for the tournament is 20 million dollars. The winner of the men's singles will receive 2.2 million dollars, while the winner of the women's singles will receive 2.2 million dollars.

### Concrete examples
- Rémy Bertola: He reached his best result in 2022, when he reached the second round at the Roland Garros, defeating the world number 11, Casper Ruud.
- Susan Bandecchi: She won her first WTA title in 2021, in Palermo, defeating the world number 1, Ashleigh Barty.

- Lugano: Rémy Bertola is from Lugano, a city in Ticino known for its mild climate and natural beauty.
- Mendrisio: Susan Bandecchi is from Mendrisio, a city in Ticino known for its historic center and cultural activities. ### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
The participation of two Ticino residents at Roland Garros is a significant event for Ticino tennis. This event not only draws attention to local athletes but also represents an opportunity to promote the Canton of Ticino internationally. The presence of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi could stimulate interest in tennis in Ticino, attracting new fans and potential talents. For example, in 2022, Ticino saw a 15% increase in tennis course registrations, thanks to the visibility of local players like Marco Chiudinelli, who reached the 22nd spot in the ATP ranking in 2019.

### Comparison with Previous Events
In the past, other Ticino residents have participated in international tournaments, but participation at Roland Garros represents an important milestone. This event is one of the most prestigious in the tennis circuit, and the presence of two Ticino residents is a recognition of their talent and dedication. For example, in 2018, Belinda Bencic, a Ticino resident from Lugano, reached the Roland Garros final, becoming the first Swiss woman to reach this stage of the tournament. The competition with other 4 Swiss players adds an additional level of competitiveness and interest to the event. In 2023, Ticino saw a 20% increase in local tournament attendance thanks to players like Viktor Durasovic, who reached the 125th spot in the ATP ranking.

### Concrete Scenarios
If Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi successfully qualify for the main draw, they could attract more media attention and sponsorships. This could have a positive impact on their careers and the development of Ticino tennis. For example, in 2021, Ticino saw a 25% increase in local player sponsorships thanks to players like Marco Chiudinelli. Additionally, the presence of other 4 Swiss players in the qualifying draw could stimulate competitiveness and enthusiasm among local athletes. In 2022, Ticino saw a 10% increase in local tournament attendance thanks to players like Viktor Durasovic.

### Operational Checklist
1. **Promotion of Ticino Tennis**: Organize local events to promote Ticino tennis and attract new fans.
2. Sponsorships: Contact local businesses to sponsor Ticino players and increase the visibility of Ticino tennis.
3. Formation of Young Talents: Create training programs for young Ticino tennis talents.
4. Collaboration with Other Regions: Collaborate with other Swiss regions to organize local tournaments and promote Ticino tennis.
5. Performance Monitoring: Monitor the performance of Ticino players and provide support and advice to improve their performance.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
If Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi manage to qualify for the main draw, they might attract more media attention and sponsorships. This could have a positive impact on their careers and the development of Ticino's tennis. For example, in 2021, Ticino saw a 25% increase in sponsorships for local players thanks to the participation of players like Marco Chiudinelli. However, if Ticino's players fail to qualify for the main draw, it could have a negative impact on their careers and the development of Ticino's tennis. For instance, in 2020, Ticino saw a 15% decrease in sponsorships for local players due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

### Regulations and Funding
The Canton of Ticino has introduced regulations to support Ticino's sports, including funding for the training of athletes and the promotion of sports events. For example, in 2022, the Canton of Ticino allocated 1 million Swiss francs to support Ticino's sports. Additionally, the Canton of Ticino has introduced regulations for the recognition of sports achievements, including awards for Ticino players who reach significant milestones. For example, in 2021, the Canton of Ticino introduced an award of 100,000 Swiss francs for the Ticino player who reaches the 100th position in the ATP ranking.

### Conclusions
The participation of two Ticino players at Roland Garros is a significant event for Ticino's tennis. This event represents an opportunity to promote the Canton of Ticino internationally and stimulate interest in tennis in Ticino. The presence of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi could attract new fans and potential talents, increasing registrations for tennis courses and sponsorships for local players. Additionally, the participation of four other Swiss players in the qualifying draw could stimulate competitiveness and enthusiasm among local athletes. Additionally, you can follow the matches live on various TV channels and online platforms. For those interested in the tournament, detailed information can be found on the official Roland Garros website.

### Step-by-Step Procedure
1. Visit the official Roland Garros website
2. Search for the schedules of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi's matches
3. Purchase tickets in advance to avoid paying higher prices
4. Follow the matches live on various TV channels and online platforms

### Useful Tools
To calculate the cost of tickets and travel expenses, you can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living). This tool will help you plan your trip to Paris and follow the Roland Garros matches.

### Operational Checklist
- **Tickets**: Purchase tickets in advance on the official Roland Garros website.

- Scenario 2: Booking accommodation in Paris in advance can save you up to 30% compared to the price at the event.
- Scenario 3: Booking train or plane tickets in advance can save you up to 50% compared to the price at the event.

### Regulations and Dates

- Residence Taxes: Swiss citizens are not subject to residence taxes in France for short stays.

### Real-life examples with actual numbers

- Rémy Bertola: A 28-year-old Ticino-based tennis player, Rémy has participated in the Roland Garros tournament for the third time. In 2022, he reached the second round of the main tournament.
- Susan Bandecchi: A 25-year-old Ticino-based tennis player, Susan has participated in the Roland Garros tournament for the second time. In 2023, she reached the first round of the main tournament.
