item.title: Ticino nurses: no reduction in working hours
item.description: The National Council has decided to keep the working week for nurses at a maximum of 50 hours, rejecting the proposal to reduce it to 45 hours.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Nurses' working week remains at 50 hours
- Proposal to reduce it to 45 hours rejected
- 14,000 vacancies in the sector

## Key facts
- **What**: Decision on the duration of nurses' working week
- When: 28 April 2026
- Where: Bern, Switzerland
- Who: Swiss National Council
- Amount: 50 hours (maximum duration of the working week)

The Swiss National Council has decided to maintain the maximum duration of the working week for nurses at 50 hours, rejecting the proposal to reduce it to 45 hours. The decision was made during a special session in Bern, where the implementation of the popular initiative 'For strong nursing care,' approved by the people in 2021, was discussed. The majority rejected the idea of shortening the working week, opting for a normal duration of 42 hours, against the 40-42 hours requested by the Government and the 38 hours demanded by the left.

The decision came after the submission of an urgent appeal signed by 190,000 people calling for the full and immediate application of the popular initiative. The debate is still ongoing, but the majority has already clearly expressed its position. Federal Councillor Elisabeth Baume-Schneider recalled that there are currently 14,000 vacancies in the nursing care sector, emphasizing the importance of improving working conditions to attract new professionals.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed in the nursing care sector in Ticino, the decision of the National Council means that the situation remains unchanged. The maximum duration of the working week will remain at 50 hours, while the normal duration will be set at 42 hours. This could influence work planning and leisure time management for many professionals in the sector.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this decision, there was a possibility that the working week would be reduced to 45 hours, which would have had a significant impact on the working conditions of nurses. The choice to maintain the current duration could be seen as a sign of stability, but also as a missed opportunity to further improve working conditions in the sector.

### What happens now
The debate on the issue of nursing care continues, and it is expected that the National Council will further delve into the issues related to working conditions and the training of professionals in the sector. For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to monitor developments and stay updated on decisions that could affect their work. ## Practical Analysis
The decision by the National Council to maintain the maximum duration of the working week for nurses at 50 hours has important implications for the nursing sector in Switzerland, and in particular for cross-border workers in Ticino. Let's take a closer look at what changes and what the practical implications are.

### Impact on Working Conditions
The decision not to shorten the working week to 45 hours means that nurses will continue to work up to 50 hours a week. This could impact fatigue and well-being of professionals, especially in a sector already under pressure due to staff shortages. The normal duration of the working week has been set at 42 hours, which represents a compromise between the demands of the Government and the left.

### Comparison with Other Professions
Compared to other professions in the healthcare sector, the duration of the working week for nurses is relatively long. For example, doctors may have more flexible hours and fewer weekly working hours, while nurses are often subject to longer and more demanding shifts. This could influence the choice of professionals to work in the nursing sector.

### Future Scenarios
If the situation remains unchanged, it is possible that the sector will continue to suffer from a shortage of staff. This could lead to increased pressure on existing staff and greater difficulty in recruiting new professionals. On the other hand, if the National Council decides to review the decision in the future, we could see an improvement in working conditions and greater interest in the profession.

### What to Do Now
For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to monitor developments and stay up-to-date on the decisions of the National Council. In case of changes, it will be necessary to adapt to the new working conditions and plan accordingly. Additionally, it is advisable to participate in initiatives and debates on the topic of nursing care to make your voice heard and contribute to improving working conditions in the sector.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Action
The decision of the National Council on the working week duration of nurses has practical implications for professionals in the sector, particularly for cross-border workers in Ticino. Let's see what to do concretely to adapt to the new working conditions and what deadlines to keep in mind.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. Stay informed about the National Council's decisions: Monitor official communications and updates on the National Council's website to stay informed about decisions related to nurses' working conditions.
2. **Adjust work planning**: If the working week duration remains at 50 hours, it is important to plan your time efficiently to manage the workload effectively.
3. Participate in initiatives and debates: Attend meetings and debates on the topic of nursing care to make your voice heard and contribute to improving working conditions in the sector.
4. Monitor job offers: Keep an eye on job offers in the nursing care sector to seize any career opportunities or improvements in working conditions.

### Important deadlines
There are no specific deadlines related to this decision, but it is important to monitor the official communications of the National Council to stay updated on any changes.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is useful to use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your budget and manage your income effectively. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website to stay updated on the latest news and job opportunities in the nursing care sector.

### Final CTA
For more information and to plan your work effectively, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the guides available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3696018)*

item.title: Andrea Giuliani leads GastroBellinzona Alto Ticino
item.description: Andrea Giuliani is the new president of GastroBellinzona Alto Ticino. The association faces economic challenges and hopes for the catering industry.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Andrea Giuliani is the new president of GastroBellinzona Alto Ticino
- The association faces economic challenges and hopes for the catering industry
- Giuliani invites members to be cautious and innovative

## Key facts
- **What**: Appointment of Andrea Giuliani as president of GastroBellinzona Alto Ticino
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Hotel Ristorante Des Alpes in Airolo
- Who: Andrea Giuliani, owner of the trattoria Er Pipa in Monte Carasso
- Amount: Not yet specified

Andrea Giuliani is the new president of GastroBellinzona Alto Ticino. The appointment took place during the association's assembly, held on April 27 at the Hotel Ristorante Des Alpes in Airolo. Giuliani, owner of the trattoria Er Pipa in Monte Carasso, succeeds Luca Merlo, to whom the section expressed heartfelt thanks for his commitment over the years both in favor of the association and, as a member of the board of directors of GastroTicino, of the cantonal federation.

During the assembly, Stefano Bernasconi of Grotto Sbardella in Giubiasco, Salvatore Squillante of Antica Osteria del Carlin in Claro, and Massimo Gandola of Locanda Marco in Bellinzona were elected to the committee. Davide Della Vecchia of Hotel Des Alpes was confirmed as treasurer, Giuseppe Luciano of the restaurant Red in Biasca, and secretary Mattia Manzocchi.

The members were also greeted by Massimo Suter, president of GastroTicino, and director Gabriele Beltrami. In his report, Giuliani outlined a balance of the past year, which he described as positive for the catering industry, especially when compared to 2024, 'a truly horrible year.' The new season presents itself 'rich in hope,' although there are several uncertainties: from the strong franc to concerns about the increase in health insurance premiums, to the consequences of the war in the Middle East, with the increase in general costs, fuel, and energy.

### Challenges and Opportunities

According to the new president, there could be a decline in activity, although international conflicts could push Swiss tourists to prefer Ticino over more distant destinations. 'I therefore invite members to be cautious, keeping their business situation under control. But also not to give up in the face of difficulties, trying to react and make their venue unique, offering their customers moments of gastronomic emotion.'

Giuliani finally underlined the willingness to continue the commitment to offer increasingly performing services, also in collaboration with GastroTicino, the political world, and the tourism sector. 'In this sense, we have continued the meetings with the various authorities and entities, participating in the working groups that have been set up from time to time, as well as we have tried to activate every possible synergy with the event organizers.' ## Analysis of Economic Challenges

The catering industry in the Canton of Ticino faces several economic challenges, including the strong franc, the increase in health insurance premiums, and the rise in general costs due to the war in the Middle East. These factors could lead to a decline in activity, but also to new opportunities.

### Impact of the Strong Franc

The strong franc represents a significant challenge for Ticino restaurateurs. With the increase in the value of the Swiss currency, the costs of imports and services rise, putting pressure on profit margins. However, the strong franc can also attract foreign tourists seeking more affordable destinations compared to other European areas.

### Increase in Health Insurance Premiums

The increase in health insurance premiums is another concern for restaurateurs. With the rise in insurance costs, restaurateurs must find ways to better manage operating expenses without compromising the quality of service. This could lead to greater attention to cost management and innovation in business models.

### Consequences of the War in the Middle East

The war in the Middle East has led to an increase in fuel and energy costs, which represent a significant portion of operating expenses for restaurateurs. However, international conflicts could also prompt Swiss tourists to prefer Ticino as a tourist destination, offering new opportunities for the catering sector.

### Growth Opportunities

Despite the challenges, there are also growth opportunities for Ticino restaurateurs. The increase in domestic tourism could lead to a greater demand for quality catering services. Furthermore, innovation and service differentiation can help restaurateurs stand out and attract a wider clientele.

### Adaptation Strategies

To address these challenges, restaurateurs must adopt adaptation strategies. This could include diversifying offerings, adopting advanced technologies for cost management, and creating unique experiences for customers. Additionally, collaboration with other companies and organizations in the sector can help create synergies and improve operational efficiency.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, the catering sector in the Canton of Ticino faces significant challenges, but also growth opportunities. With careful and strategic management, restaurateurs can overcome difficulties and seize the new opportunities that arise.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete Actions for Restaurateurs

To tackle economic challenges and seize growth opportunities, Ticino restaurateurs can adopt various concrete actions. Here are some practical strategies:

### Cost Monitoring

1. **Cost Analysis**: Conduct a detailed analysis of operating costs to identify areas where expenses can be reduced.
2. Procurement Control: Negotiate with suppliers to obtain better purchasing conditions and reduce the cost of raw materials.
3. Energy Management: Implement solutions to reduce energy consumption, such as using low-consumption appliances and adopting renewable energy sources.

### Innovation and Differentiation

1. Diversified Menu: Offer a diversified menu that meets the needs of a varied clientele, including options for different diets and food preferences.
2. Unique Experiences: Create unique experiences for customers, such as themed dinners, special events, and collaborations with local chefs.
3. Advanced Technology: Adopt advanced technologies to improve operational efficiency, such as online booking management systems and cost management software.

### Collaboration and Synergies

1. Strategic Partnerships: Collaborate with other companies and organizations in the sector to create synergies and improve operational efficiency.
2. Event Participation: Participate in trade fairs and industry events to promote your restaurant and create networks of contacts.
3. Shared Promotion: Collaborate with other restaurateurs to promote the restaurant sector in the Canton of Ticino through joint marketing campaigns.

### Use of Support Tools

1. Cost Calculator: Use tools like the [cost calculator](nav:calculator) to monitor and manage operating expenses.
2. Guides and Resources: Consult online guides and resources to get advice on how to improve restaurant management.
3. Professional Support: Seek support from consultants and industry professionals to help manage economic challenges.

### Conclusion

By adopting these concrete strategies, Ticino restaurateurs can tackle economic challenges and seize growth opportunities. The key to success lies in careful cost management, innovation, and collaboration with other companies and organizations in the sector. By using support tools and available resources, restaurateurs can improve operational efficiency and offer unique experiences to their customers.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921518/andrea-giuliani-alla-guida-di-gastrobellinzona-alto-ticino)*

item.title: 139 Ticino cross-border athletes at Special Olympics National Summer Games
item.description: 139 athletes with intellectual disabilities from Ticino will participate in the Special Olympics National Summer Games in Zug from May 28 to 31, 2026.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 139 athletes from Ticino at the National Summer Games
- 1600 total participants with intellectual disabilities
- 16 sports on the program

## Key facts
- **What**: National Summer Games of Special Olympics
- When: 28-31 May 2026
- Where: Zug, Switzerland
- Who: Athletes with intellectual disabilities, including 139 from Ticino
- Amount: 1600 total participants

From 28 to 31 May 2026, Zug becomes the heart of inclusive sports in Switzerland, hosting the National Summer Games of Special Olympics Switzerland. Athletes from Italian-speaking Switzerland, including 139 participants from Ticino, join over 1600 competitors for four days of sports, inclusion, and celebration. Competitions will take place in 16 disciplines, including athletics, basketball, bocce, golf, artistic gymnastics, pétanque, sailing, swimming, tennis, and table tennis.

The event, held every two years alternating between summer and winter editions, is the largest of its kind in Switzerland for people with intellectual disabilities. In addition to the athletes, 600 coaches and delegations from 11 countries will be present, including Austria, France, Germany, Gibraltar, Italy, Liechtenstein, Monaco, Poland, San Marino, Ukraine, and Uzbekistan.

The opening ceremony will take place on Thursday, 28 May, with a parade of athletes, the Special Olympics anthem, and the lighting of the 'flame of hope.' Among the expected guests is Federal Councillor and Minister of Sport Martin Pfister, while singer Marc Sway will present a song created for the occasion. The ceremony will be hosted by Christa Rigozzi and Nik Hartmann.

### The philosophy of Special Olympics

At the heart of the event is the philosophy of Special Olympics, which emphasizes individual abilities and personal commitment. The goal is not to compete with the best, but to promote the development of each athlete. To ensure balanced competitions, the divisioning system is applied: athletes are divided into homogeneous groups based on their results, with performance differences kept within predetermined limits. Each group competes with three to eight participants, ensuring equal opportunities for success.

### The competition schedule

The schedule includes divisioning competitions on 28 May and, from 5:00 PM, the opening ceremony at the OYM hall. On 29 and 30 May, competitions will take place alongside activities in the Olympic village and award ceremonies. On 31 May, the final competitions are scheduled, followed by the closing ceremony from 3:00 PM.

The organization of the Games is entrusted to the local committee, committed to creating an event that unites sports, inclusion, and community participation. The collateral program offers the population various opportunities to experience the event up close. The National Games also represent a possible stepping stone to the World Games in Santiago, Chile, in 2027: for many athletes, participation is an important step in their sports journey. ### Implications for Ticino Cross-Border Workers

The participation of 139 athletes from Ticino in the National Summer Games of Special Olympics represents an important opportunity to promote inclusion and visibility of sports for people with intellectual disabilities. For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Switzerland, this event can be an occasion to actively participate as volunteers or simply to support local athletes.

### How to Participate as a Volunteer

If you are a cross-border worker interested in participating as a volunteer, you can contact the local organizing committee for information on how to join the team of volunteers. The event involves up to 1500 volunteers and offers a unique opportunity to contribute to an inclusive and meaningful sporting event.

### Side Activities and Olympic Village

In addition to the sporting competitions, the Olympic Village set up at the OYM hall will offer interactive activities, stands, and refreshment points. The proximity between the competition venues favors the meeting between athletes, the public, and volunteers, contributing to creating a cohesive and participatory atmosphere. This is an excellent way for cross-border workers to experience the Games up close and support Ticino athletes.

### Support for Families

The event also includes a Family Program designed for about 200 family members, with moments of exchange and support. This program offers a valuable opportunity for the families of athletes to actively participate and share the experience of the Games.

### Opportunities for Athletes

For many athletes, participation in the National Games represents an important step towards the World Games in Santiago, Chile 2027. This event can be a springboard for their sporting career, offering them the opportunity to compete at an international level and improve their sporting skills.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate or support the event

If you are a cross-border worker interested in participating or supporting the National Summer Games of Special Olympics, here are some concrete steps you can follow:

1. **Inform yourself**: Visit the official website of Special Olympics Switzerland to get updated information about the event and volunteering opportunities.
2. Contact the organizing committee: If you are interested in becoming a volunteer, contact the local committee to find out how to join the team.
3. Participate in side activities: Visit the Olympic village and participate in interactive activities and side events to experience the Games up close.
4. Support the athletes: Attend the award ceremonies and competitions to support Ticino athletes and celebrate their successes.
5. Spread the news: Share information about the event with friends and family to promote inclusion and sports for people with intellectual disabilities.

### Useful tools

For further information and to plan your participation, you can use the [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate travel and accommodation expenses, and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to verify the requirements for participating as a volunteer.

### Conclusion

The National Summer Games of Special Olympics represent a unique opportunity to promote inclusion and sports for people with intellectual disabilities. For cross-border workers in Ticino, this event offers a valuable opportunity to actively participate as volunteers, support local athletes, and experience the Games up close. Don't miss the opportunity to be part of this extraordinary event and contribute to a more inclusive and supportive world.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921483/anche-139-partecipanti-della-svizzera-italiana-al-national-summer-games-di-special-olympics)*

item.title: Ex Bank of Italy HQ for sale in Varese: why it's not selling
item.description: The former headquarters of the Bank of Italy in Varese has been for sale for years. We discover the price and the reasons why it doesn't find buyers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Former Bank of Italy headquarters for sale in Varese
- Price not specified, but hard to sell
- Rain returns interrupt early summer

## Key facts
- **What**: Former headquarters of the Bank of Italy
- When: For sale for years
- Where: Varese, Italy
- Who: Owner not specified
- Amount: Price not yet specified

The former headquarters of the Bank of Italy in Varese, a large building that occupies a central part of the city, has been for sale for years. Despite its strategic position, the large building has not found buyers. The editorial staff of VareseNews has dealt with this case, trying to understand the reason for the difficulty in selling.

The building, which until ten years ago housed a territorial headquarters of the Bank of Italy, is an important piece of the center of Varese. Its sale could represent an opportunity for urban renewal, but the unspecified price and market conditions could be significant obstacles.

In addition to the real estate issue, the article also mentions the return of the rain, which has interrupted the early summer. This weather event has brought a break from the heat, but has not directly influenced the situation of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy.

### Analysis of the real estate market
To better understand the situation, it is useful to analyze the real estate market of Varese. The city, located in a strategic position near the border with Switzerland, attracts many cross-border workers who work in the Canton of Ticino. This flow of cross-border workers could influence the demand for real estate, but the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy, given its size and intended use, may not be easily adaptable to the needs of the market.

### Comparison with other real estate sales
A comparison with other large real estate sales in Varese could help better understand the value of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy. However, without specific data on the price and sales conditions, it is difficult to make a detailed analysis. It is possible that the asking price is too high compared to the market value, or that there are urban planning constraints that limit the possibilities of renovation and reuse.

### Implications for the future
The sale of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy could have significant implications for the future of Varese. If the building remains unsold, it could become a symbol of economic difficulties and lack of vision for urban renewal. On the other hand, if it finds a buyer willing to invest in its renovation, it could become a reference point for the city.

### Conclusion
The former headquarters of the Bank of Italy in Varese represents a challenge for the local real estate market. Its sale could bring significant benefits, but the current difficulties require an in-depth analysis and a targeted strategy to attract investors. ## Analysis of sales difficulties
The difficulty in selling the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy in Varese could be due to several factors. One of the main ones is the size of the building. A large building requires a significant investment for renovation and adaptation to new market needs. This could deter potential buyers, especially in an uncertain economic context.

### Urban planning constraints
Another factor that could influence the sale is the presence of urban planning constraints. The former headquarters of the Bank of Italy may be subject to constraints that limit the possibilities for renovation and change of use. These constraints can make the building less attractive to investors, who may prefer properties without such limitations.

### Comparisons with other cities
A comparison with other Italian cities could help better understand the situation in Varese. For example, in other Italian cities, large buildings have been renovated and converted into shopping centers, offices or residences. However, without specific data on market conditions and sale prices, it is difficult to make a detailed analysis.

### Possible solutions
To overcome the difficulties in selling, it may be necessary to adopt a targeted strategy. For example, the municipality of Varese could offer tax incentives to attract investors. Moreover, it could be useful to promote the building as an investment opportunity, highlighting the advantages of its central location and the potential development opportunities.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The former headquarters of the Bank of Italy could also have implications for cross-border workers who work in the Canton of Ticino. If the building were renovated and converted into offices or residences, it could attract new cross-border workers, contributing to the economic growth of the city. However, without a clear plan for renovation and reuse, these opportunities could remain unexplored.

### Conclusion
The sale of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy in Varese represents a complex challenge, which requires an in-depth analysis and a targeted strategy. Current difficulties could be overcome with the adoption of incentives and the promotion of the building as an investment opportunity.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to attract investors
To attract investors and facilitate the sale of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy, it is necessary to adopt a series of concrete measures. Here are some steps that could be useful:

1. **Detailed market analysis**: Conduct a detailed market analysis to understand the needs and preferences of potential investors. This could include surveys, interviews, and market studies.
2. Promote the building: Promote the building as an investment opportunity, highlighting the advantages of its central location and potential development opportunities. This could include advertising campaigns, presentations, and guided tours.
3. Tax incentives: Offer tax incentives to attract investors. This could include tax reductions, financial benefits, and other advantages.
4. Collaboration with the municipality: Collaborate with the municipality of Varese to develop a renovation and reuse plan for the building. This could include reviewing urban planning constraints and promoting development projects.
5. Community involvement: Involve the local community in the sales and renovation process. This could include public consultations, meetings with residents, and the promotion of projects that benefit the community.

### Useful tools
To facilitate the sale and renovation of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy, various tools and resources could be useful. For example, the salary calculator could help estimate the costs and benefits of the renovation. Additionally, the permit comparator could be useful for understanding the regulations and requirements for the renovation.

### Conclusion
The sale of the former headquarters of the Bank of Italy in Varese represents a complex challenge, but with a targeted strategy and the adoption of concrete measures, it could be possible to attract investors and facilitate the renovation of the building. Collaboration with the municipality, promotion of the building, and community involvement are fundamental steps to achieve this goal.

item.title: Saronno: new rules for summer centres
item.description: From 2026, Saronno introduces a single management for summer centres, discounts for siblings and contributions up to 400 euros for families with ISEE up to 15,000 euros.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Single management for summer centers ages 3-14
- Discounts for siblings even in different age groups
- Contributions up to 400 euros for ISEE up to 15,000 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: New rules for municipal summer centers
- When: Starting July 2026
- Where: Don Marzorati, Monte Santo, and Damiano Chiesa schools in Saronno
- Who: Municipality of Saronno
- Amount: Contributions up to 400 euros per child

The Municipality of Saronno has introduced new rules for municipal summer centers starting in 2026. The main novelty is the introduction of a single management system for all age groups, from 3 to 14 years old. This model was developed in collaboration with the Monsignor Zerbi Municipal Institution, with the aim of ensuring educational continuity and simplifying management for families. The organization will be homogeneous, with the extension of pre and post-center services to all ages and greater flexibility in attendance.

Among the new measures introduced, there is also a much-anticipated measure for many families: the 'siblings' discount will also apply in cases where children attend summer centers of different age groups, such as early childhood and primary. The Municipality has allocated dedicated funds to support access to services. In particular, contributions of up to 400 euros per child are provided for families with an ISEE of up to 15,000 euros. This is in addition to an allocation of 14,000 euros destined for inclusion, to cover the costs of specialized educational assistance. The contribution will also be valid for participation in summer centers throughout the national territory.

The municipal summer centers will be active in the month of July at three locations: Don Marzorati, Monte Santo, and Damiano Chiesa schools. It will be possible to enroll on a weekly basis, to ensure maximum flexibility. The enrollment procedures and contribution request methods will be available shortly on the Municipality's website.

### Discounts and Contributions
The new rules introduce significant discounts for families. The 'siblings' discount has been extended to cases where children attend summer centers of different age groups. This is a great advantage for families with more than one child, who can benefit from reductions even if the children are in different age groups. Furthermore, the Municipality has provided contributions of up to 400 euros per child for families with an ISEE of up to 15,000 euros. This contribution is also valid for participation in summer centers throughout the national territory, making the offer even more inclusive.

### Inclusion and Specialized Assistance
Another important aspect of the new rules is the attention to inclusion. The Municipality has allocated 14,000 euros for specialized educational assistance. This contribution is aimed at covering the costs of assistance for children who need additional support during summer centers. This is an important step towards a more inclusive and accessible educational offer for all.

### Enrollment Procedures
The enrollment procedures and contribution request methods will be available shortly on the Municipality's website. The summer centers will be active in the month of July, and it will be possible to enroll on a weekly basis. This flexibility allows families to best organize their children's participation in summer centers, adapting to their specific needs.

### Conclusions
The new rules for the municipal summer centers of Saronno represent a significant step forward towards a more homogeneous, inclusive, and flexible educational offer. Thanks to the single management, sibling discounts, and economic contributions, families will be able to benefit from quality services at advantageous conditions. The enrollment procedures and contribution request methods will be available shortly on the Municipality's website, making the access process simple and transparent. The new rules for the municipal summer centers in Saronno introduce a series of significant changes that will have a positive impact on families. The unified management for all age groups, from 3 to 14 years old, ensures educational continuity and simplifies management for families. This model, developed in collaboration with the Monsignor Zerbi municipal institution, represents a step forward towards a more homogeneous and inclusive educational offer.

### Implications for Families
The new rules introduce significant discounts for families. The 'siblings' discount has been extended to cases where children attend summer centers for different age groups. This is a great advantage for families with more than one child, who can benefit from reductions even if the children are in different age groups. Additionally, the Municipality has provided contributions of up to 400 euros per child for families with an ISEE of up to 15,000 euros. This contribution is also valid for participation in summer centers throughout the national territory, making the offer even more inclusive.

### Inclusion and Specialized Assistance
Another important aspect of the new rules is the attention to inclusion. The Municipality has allocated 14,000 euros for specialized educational assistance. This contribution is intended to cover the costs of assistance for children who need additional support during summer centers. This is an important step towards a more inclusive and accessible educational offer for everyone.

### Enrollment Procedures
The enrollment procedures and the request for contributions will be available shortly on the Municipality's website. The summer centers will be active in the month of July and it will be possible to enroll on a weekly basis. This flexibility allows families to best organize their children's participation in the summer centers, adapting to their specific needs.

### Comparisons with the Previous Situation
Before the introduction of the new rules, the municipal summer centers in Saronno were managed in a fragmented way, with different rules and rates for different age groups. This could create confusion and difficulties for families, especially those with more than one child. With the new unified management, all age groups will be managed in a homogeneous way, simplifying the management and making the offer more transparent and accessible.

### Practical Scenarios
Imagine a family with two children, one 5 years old and the other 10 years old. Before the new rules, the two children would have had to enroll in different summer centers, with different rates and rules. With the new unified management, the two children can attend the same summer center, benefiting from the same services and rates. Additionally, thanks to the 'siblings' discount, the family can benefit from a reduction in the total enrollment cost.

### Conclusions
The new rules for the municipal summer centers in Saronno represent a significant step forward towards a more homogeneous, inclusive, and flexible educational offer. Thanks to the unified management, discounts for siblings, and economic contributions, families can benefit from quality services at advantageous conditions. The enrollment procedures and the request for contributions will be available shortly on the Municipality's website, making the access process simple and transparent.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). To take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the municipal summer centers in Saronno, families must follow a series of simple and clear steps. Here is a step-by-step guide for registration and applying for contributions.

### Step 1: Verify Requirements
Before proceeding with registration, it is important to verify the requirements to access economic contributions. Families with an ISEE up to 15,000 euros will be able to benefit from contributions up to 400 euros per minor. Additionally, it is possible to take advantage of the 'siblings' discount even if the children attend summer centers of different age groups.

### Step 2: Online Registration
The registration methods will be available soon on the website of the Municipality of Saronno. Families will be able to register online, filling out the registration form and providing all the required information. It is important to ensure that correct and complete data is entered to avoid delays in the registration process.

### Step 3: Applying for Contributions
After registration, families can apply for economic contributions. This procedure will also be available online, through the Municipality's website. It will be necessary to provide the required documentation, such as the ISEE certificate and any attendance certificates from previous summer centers.

### Step 4: Confirmation and Payment
Once the application is completed, the Municipality will evaluate the documentation and confirm eligibility for the contribution. Families will receive an official communication with all the necessary information for payment. It is important to keep this communication for any future verifications.

### Step 5: Participation in Summer Centers
With registration completed and the contribution confirmed, families can finally enroll their children in the municipal summer centers. The centers will be active in the month of July and it will be possible to register on a weekly basis, ensuring maximum flexibility.

### Useful Tools
To facilitate the registration process and the application for contributions, the Municipality of Saronno has made a series of useful tools available. Among these, an online calculator to estimate the contribution to which one is entitled based on the ISEE and a detailed guide with all the necessary information to complete the procedures.

### Conclusions
The new rules for the municipal summer centers in Saronno represent an important opportunity for families. Thanks to the unitary management, discounts for siblings, and economic contributions, families will be able to take advantage of quality services at advantageous conditions. By following the indicated steps, families will be able to complete the registration and the application for contributions in a simple and transparent manner, ensuring their children a summer full of fun and learning.

For further information and to access the online services, visit the website of the Municipality of Saronno. [Find out more](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/a-saronno-nuove-regole-per-i-centri-estivi-comunali-regia-unica-sconti-e-contributi-fino-a-400-euro/2559907/)*

item.title: Former army chief joins Kaiser Partner Privatbank board
item.description: Thomas Süssli, former Swiss army chief, joins the board of Vaduz-based bank.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Former Swiss army chief joins Kaiser Partner Privatbank board
- Süssli brings experience in economy, security, and technology
- Bank highlights importance of geopolitical and regulatory risks

## Key facts
- **What**: Appointment of Thomas Süssli to Kaiser Partner Privatbank board
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Vaduz, Liechtenstein
- Who: Thomas Süssli, former Swiss army chief
- Amount: Not yet specified

Former Swiss army chief Thomas Süssli has joined the board of directors of Kaiser Partner Privatbank in Vaduz. The appointment was announced by the Liechtenstein-based bank, which emphasized Süssli's extensive executive experience at the intersection of economy, security, and technology. Süssli also has experience in the financial sector, as well as in strategy, digitalization, and cybersecurity.

The private bank of the Principality highlighted the growing importance of geopolitical risks, technological developments, and regulatory requirements for financial service providers. Fritz Kaiser, head of the Fritz Kaiser Group, expressed satisfaction with the collaboration with Süssli, describing him as a personality with strategic foresight and extensive executive experience.

Süssli, 59, was the chief of the Swiss army from 2020 to 2025. Recently, he announced his intention to run for the National Council with the PLR, representing the canton of Lucerne. At the end of November, he handed over the reins of the Swiss armed forces to Benedikt Roos.

Süssli's appointment could have implications for cross-border workers using the services of Kaiser Partner Privatbank. ### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who wish to deepen their financial knowledge, Kaiser Partner Privatbank may offer useful tools and resources. For example, the bank may provide guides and tutorials on how to best manage your savings and investments. Additionally, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an estimate of their income and the taxes they need to pay.

### Conclusion

The appointment of Thomas Süssli to the board of directors of Kaiser Partner Privatbank represents an opportunity for cross-border workers to benefit from safer and more innovative financial services. By following the steps described, cross-border workers can open an account and use the services offered by the bank, ensuring more efficient and secure financial management.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/economia/1921506/s-ssli-kaiser-capo-esercito-privatbank-partner)*

item.title: Cardiocentro Lugano: three more floors and a panoramic bar
item.description: The Cardiocentro in Lugano unveils three additional floors and a panoramic bar after three years of work. Comfort and breathtaking views for patients and staff.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Cardiocentro Lugano: three more floors and a panoramic bar
- New bright and comfortable spaces for patients and staff
- 28 rooms, including 3 private ones per department

## Key facts
- **What**: Renovation of Cardiocentro Lugano
- When: Works completed in 2026
- Where: Lugano, Canton Ticino
- Who: Cardiocentro Ticino Institute of EOC
- Amount: Three more floors and a panoramic bar

Lugano, April 28, 2026 - After three years of work, Cardiocentro Lugano unveils its new face, characterized by brightness, comfort, and functionality. The structure, which never interrupted its activity during the works, has been enriched with three new floors and a panoramic bar called Baricentro.

The director of Cardiocentro, Massimo Manserra, explained that the new spaces include two floors dedicated to patient care and administrative areas, while the third floor hosts the panoramic bar. 'The construction site, although significant, never stopped the activity of the structure,' Manserra emphasized.

From an assistential point of view, the renovation has brought a concrete improvement in the quality of the environments. 'The new spaces have certainly brought more light and more comfort to the nurses and, in particular, to our patients,' declared Stefano Bernasconi, head of the nursing service. Currently, there are 28 rooms, including 11 shared and semi-private, and three private ones per department.

### Patients' perception

The change has also been perceived by the patients. 'Patients are certainly quite amazed because it doesn't seem like being inside a hospital,' added Bernasconi. For the staff, the new context also represents a stimulus to do better.

The head of cardiology, Marco Valgimigli, highlighted how the change is mainly perceptual. 'Formally, nothing has changed in the way we approach patients. It is perhaps our perception, and I hope that of the patients themselves, due to the fact that they are welcomed in a new, clean environment, where someone is always concerned with maintaining hyper-sophisticated and hyper-welcoming spaces.'

Valgimigli also emphasized the value of institutional support. 'The fact that there is continuous care, continuous concern not only from professionals, but also from politicians, from those who govern us, from the resources that the Municipality of Lugano and the Canton of Ticino make available, gives the sense of continuous growth towards an excellence that we will certainly never reach, but it is still a continuous attempt.'

### The Baricentro

A symbolic element of the renovation is the Baricentro, located on the roof of the structure and designed as a meeting and break place. 'It is exactly this, even when we thought about it, we dreamed it this way,' recounted Manserra. 'Having the privilege of being in a wonderful position with a view of the mountains of Lugano, the lake, the entire panorama.'

The bar is open not only to the staff but also to patients and their families. 'A place where one gathers, meets for a moment of pause, which can be for all collaborators, but also for patients who can perhaps go up with their loved ones and enjoy a coffee or a good lunch in a place that makes you think of something else, not necessarily being in a hospital,' concluded the director. The renovation of the Cardiocentro in Lugano represents a significant step towards improving working and recovery conditions for both patients and staff. The new bright and comfortable spaces not only enhance the quality of life within the facility but also provide a more welcoming and relaxing environment.

### Implications for Patients

Patients benefit from a more comfortable and welcoming environment, which can contribute to better recovery. The presence of private and semi-private rooms offers greater privacy and comfort, while the new common areas promote social interaction and support among patients.

### Impact on Staff

For medical and nursing staff, the new spaces represent a significant improvement in working conditions. The increased brightness and comfort of the environments can reduce stress and increase productivity. Furthermore, the new setting encourages staff to perform better, improving the quality of care provided.

### The Role of Institutions

The support of local institutions, such as the Municipality of Lugano and the Canton of Ticino, has been fundamental for the realization of this project. The continuous investment in resources and infrastructure demonstrates a commitment to excellence in the healthcare sector. This support not only improves the quality of services offered but also contributes to the growth and development of the community.

### The Baricentro: A Meeting Place

The Baricentro, with its panoramic view, represents a meeting and relaxation spot for everyone who frequents the facility. It offers an opportunity to unwind and enjoy a moment of leisure, away from the hospital environment. This space is open to patients, family members, and staff, promoting a sense of community and sharing.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For patients and relatives who wish to visit the Cardiocentro in Lugano, it is important to know the procedures and regulations in place. Here are some steps and useful information:

### Visiting procedures

1. **Booking**: Contact the Cardiocentro to book an appointment. This can be done by phone or through the facility's website.
2. Documentation: Bring all necessary medical documentation, including reports and prescriptions.
3. Visiting hours: Check the visiting hours set by the Cardiocentro. Usually, visits are allowed at specific times to ensure the comfort and privacy of all patients.

### Useful information

- Address: Cardiocentro Ticino, Via Tesserete 48, 6900 Lugano
- Phone: +41 91 811 81 11
- Website: [www.eoc.ch](https://www.eoc.ch)

### Useful tools

For more information and to plan your visit, you can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate the expenses related to your stay in Lugano. Additionally, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you better understand your expenses and income.

### Conclusion

The renovation of the Cardiocentro in Lugano represents an important step forward for the quality of healthcare in the Canton of Ticino. With its new bright and comfortable spaces, the Cardiocentro offers a welcoming and relaxing environment for patients and staff. Don't forget to use the tools available on our website to plan your visit and understand your expenses.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921278/comfort-ambienti-top-e-panorami-mozzafiato-qui-si-cura-il-cuore-dei-ticinesi)*
